observations.title: T1 の deps 注入手順が若干暗黙的
observations.rationale: T1 の「deps 注入で状態遷移を再現」は、scan override で orphan を返させつつ再チェック用の fs.readFile を active state.json を返すよう mock するという手順を想定していると読める。SidecarPruneFs が SidecarScanFs を満たす構造上、追加 deps なしで実現可能であり技術的問題はないが、実装者向けにテストコメントで意図を補足すると確実。

toolResult.reason:
Wrote design.md, spec.md, and tasks.md under specrunner/changes/prune-force-recheck-before-delete/ on branch change/prune-force-recheck-before-delete-c193e3e3.

Design summary:
- D1: Add an injectable per-slug re-check (RecheckSidecarFn, mirroring isOrphanSidecar) to pruneOrphanSidecars; the --force loop calls it immediately before each fs.rm and skips deletion when the slug is no longer an orphan (became active after scan). Reuses the shared isOrphanSidecar predicate (single source of truth).
- Wiring: runPrune (the only production caller) injects recheck: isOrphanSidecar. The runner's default (no injection) trusts the scan classification — a deliberate choice because isOrphanSidecar returns false for both "active" and "no state.json", so a safe-by-default fs-reading recheck would break the ~7 existing --force runner tests that stub fs minimally. The production wiring is guarded by a CLI test (deps.recheck === isOrphanSidecar) so it can't silently regress; the runner skip logic is pinned by the T1 破壊確認.
- D2: A skip is warning-level + exit 0 (never a failure); "Removed N" counts only actual deletions; re-check rejection is fail-safe (skip, exit 0).
- D3: No cross-command lock introduced. Documented the residual read→rm window and its bounded, self-healing impact (only machine-local liveness.json lost; authoritative branch-borne state survives; isStaleRunning misjudges a live job as stale until the sidecar is re-materialized). Lock deferred as a named follow-up.
- D4: Dry-run and worktree prune untouched.

tasks.md: T-01 runner re-check, T-02 CLI wiring, T-03 race/破壊確認/no-false-skip tests, T-04 preserve existing tests + wiring assertion, T-05 full gate. All acceptance criteria (T1–T4) mapped. No source edits performed — planning only.
